verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a 9-pass transmembrane autophagy protein resident in the trans-Golgi network and recycling endosomes. Despite multi-pass topology, it remains restricted to the endomembrane system and does not traffic to the plasma membrane even under autophagy induction. No documented PM pool, no ectodomain shedding, and no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targeting. Ruled out: cell_state_induced (autophagy triggers intracellular cycling only, not PM translocation), tissue_restricted_surface (ubiquitous), lysosomal_exocytosis (not lysosomal resident), dual_localization (no PM pool documented), stable_surface_attachment (not secreted).
